questions.question_text: Messengers were sent throughout the kingdom

options.label: B
options.text: to find answers to the questions

options.label: C
options.text: to look for the wise hermit

options.label: D
options.text: to announce a reward for those who could answer the questions

questions.correct_answer: to announce a reward for those who could answer the questions
questions.explanation_text: to announce a reward for those who could answer the questions

questions.question_text: The hermit drew the king's attention

options.label: D
options.text: to the seeds to be sown

questions.question_text: The king was not satisfied with the answers as:
questions.explanation_text: As the answers to his questions were so different, the king was not satisfied.

questions.question_text: Why did the king get off his horse to visit the hermit?

options.label: A
options.text: The hermit received only common folks

options.label: B
options.text: The hermit did not like animals

options.label: C
options.text: The king wanted to see the hermit secretly

questions.correct_answer: The hermit received only common folks
questions.explanation_text: The king got off his horse and went on foot to meet the hermit for he was well aware of the fact that the hermit received only common folks, so, to appear like a commoner he left his horse with his bodyguard and went on alone.

questions.question_text: Who wounded the bearded man in the plot, Three questions?
questions.explanation_text: The bearded man was following the king to kill him to avenge his brother's death but the king's bodyguard spotted him and wounded him on recognition.

questions.question_text: The bearded man in lesson Three Questions was
How did the hermit answer the king's questions?

options.label: A
options.text: By creating an opportunity to help another

options.label: B
options.text: By illustrating the importance of hard work

options.label: C
options.text: By showing the value of forgiveness

options.label: D
options.text: By referring to a shared experience

questions.correct_answer: By referring to a shared experience
questions.explanation_text: By referring to a shared experience

questions.question_text: What did the bearded man do as he approached the king? (Three questions)

options.label: A
options.text: He fell fainting on the ground

questions.correct_answer: He fell fainting on the ground
questions.explanation_text: The bearded man was badly injured and as he reached the king, he fainted and fell to the ground.

questions.question_text: The bearded man asked for ________.
questions.explanation_text: The bearded man felt grateful to the king who had saved his life and asked for forgiveness ​​​​​​​because he had actually come to kill the king.

questions.question_text: What were the questions to which the king wanted answers in the plot, Three questions?

options.label: A
options.text: What is the right time to begin an action?

options.label: B
options.text: Who were the people to whom he should listen?

options.label: C
options.text: What is the most important thing for him to do?

questions.explanation_text: The king wanted answers to the three questions for he believed that knowing them would improvise him as a ruler and would help him to reach pinnacles of success.

questions.question_text:
'I came to you, wise man, for an answer to my questions. If you can give me no answer, tell me so and I will return home'.
How is the king most likely feeling when he says this?
What did the king offer to give to the person who would answer his three questions?

options.label: B
options.text: A part of his kingdom to rule

options.label: C
options.text: The person would be appointed as king's chief councilor

options.label: D
options.text: He would make the person his successor

questions.explanation_text: The king sent his messengers throughout his kingdom, promising a large sum of money to anyone who would answer his three questions.

questions.question_text: What did the king and hermit do on seeing the injured man in the plot, Three questions?
questions.explanation_text: The king and the hermit immediately took action on seeing blood flowing from the injured man's stomach. They hurriedly unfastened his clothes and attended his wound.

questions.question_text: Which decision protected the king's life?
Who was the wounded man?
questions.explanation_text: The wounded told the king that he was his enemy.

questions.question_text: Why did the bearded man press his stomach with his hand? (Three Questions)

Each of the following sentences has two blanks. Fill in the blanks with appropriate forms (Noun/Verb) of the word given in brackets: Eg:
He has ________ to help me. Do you think he will remember his ________? (promise)
He has promised to help me. Do you think he will remember his promise?
The ________ said that only fresh evidence would make him change his ________. (judge)
I didn't notice any serious ________ of opinion among the debaters, although they ________ from one another over small points. (differ)
It's a fairly simple question to ________, but will you accept my ________ as final? (answer)
It isn't ________ that ________ should always be the mother of invention. (necessary)
Hermits are ________ men. How they acquire their ________ no one can tell. (wise)
The committee has ________ to make Jagdish captain of the team. The ________ is likely to please everyone. (decide)
Asking for ________ is as noble as willingness to ________. (forgive)
Why did he ask for the king's forgiveness? Answer in the content of Three questions.
Why were the messengers sent throughout the kingdom in the story Three Questions
The king forgave the bearded man. What did he do to show his forgiveness? Answer in the content of Three Questions.
What advice was given to the king regarding the second question? (Three Questions)
Did the wise men win the reward? If not, why not? Answer in the context of Three questions.
Who was the bearded man? Answer in the context of Three questions.
Why did many wise men come to the king? Answer in the context of Three Questions.
In answer to the second question, whose advice did the people say would be important to the king? [Three Questions]
What were the hermit's answers to the three questions? Write each answer separately. Which answer do you like most, and why?
What suggestions were made in answer to the third question? [Three Questions]
Why was the king advised to go to magicians? [Three Questions]
How did the king and the hermit help the wounded man? [Three Questions]

Imagine you are the hermit. Write briefly the incident of your meeting the king. Begin like this:
One day I was digging in my garden. A man in ordinary clothes came to see me. I knew it was the king...
Why did the king want to know answers to [Three Questions]
Why was the king advised to listen to his soldiers in the story Three Questions?
Was the king vengeful? Give a reason for your answer.

Complete the following sentences by adding the appropriate parts of the sentences given in the box.
Many wise men answered the king's questions, ________
Someone suggested that there should be a council of wise men ________
Someone else suggested that the king should have a timetable ________
The king requested the hermit ________
The king washed and dressed the bearded man's wound, ________





but the bleeding would not stop.
to answer three questions.
but their answers were so varied that the king was not satisfied.
and follow it strictly.
to help the king act at the right time.
Describe the king in your own words. Write your answer in the context of Three Questions.

Match items in List A with their meanings in List B.
fainted: lost consciousness


A
B


(i) wounded
got up from sleep


(ii) awoke
give back


(iii) forgive
small patches of ground for plants


(iv) faithful
severely injured


(v) pity
pardon


(vi) beds
loyal


(vii) return
feel sorry for


Use any three of the above words in sentences of your own. You may change the form of the word.
Why did the king change his clothes and leave behind his bodyguards and horse before meeting the hermit in the story Three Questions?
How did the bearded man turn from the king's enemy to his friend? Answer in the content of Three Questions.
Why did the king refuse to give reward to anyone in the story Three Questions?
Do you think the hermit realised his visitor was a king? Give a reason for your answer.

Imagine you are the king. Narrate the incident of your meeting the hermit. Begin like this:
The wise men answered my questions, but I was not satisfied with their answers. One day I decided to go and meet the hermit...
What were the replies the king received for his first question? Write your answer in the context of Three Questions.

Read the following passages carefully and answer the questions that follow each of them: (1x5=5)
The thought came to a certain king that he would never fail if he knew three things. These three things were: What is the right time to begin something? Which people should he listen to? What is the most important thing for him to do?
The king, therefore, sent messengers throughout his kingdom, promising a large sum of money to anyone who would answer these three questions. Many wise men came to the king, but they all answered his questions differently.
Name the story and its author.
What were the king's questions?
Why was he keen to get the answers?
Who did he call to the court to answer his questions?
Pick out a word from the above passage that means opposite to foolish.

Read the passage and answer the following questions:
The answers to his questions were so different, that the king was not satisfied and gave no reward. Instead, he decided to seek the advice of a hermit, who was widely known for his wisdom. The hermit lived in a hut which he never left.
Why was the reward not given to any of the wise men?
Finally, the king decided to meet whom? Where did he live?
The hermit was famous for what?
Pick out a word which is the opposite of:

Ignorance
Similar

Read the following passage carefully and answer the questions that follow each of them:(1x5=5)
"You do not know me, but I know you. I am that enemy of yours who swore revenge on you because you put my brother to death and seized my property. I knew you had gone alone to see that hermit, and I made up my mind to kill you on your way home. But the day passed and you did not return. So I left my hiding-place, and I came upon your bodyguard, who recognised me and wounded me. I escaped from him but I should have died if you had not dressed my wounds. I wished to kill you, and you have saved my life. Now, if I live, I will serve you as your most faithful servant and will order my sons to do the same. Forgive me!"
Who does me refer to?
Why did the man want to kill the king?
How did the king help the man?
What did the king's bodyguard do to him?
Pick out a word from the above passage that means the same as identified.

Read the following passage carefully and answer the questions:
When the king had dug two beds, he stopped and repeated his questions. The hermit gave no answer, but stood up, stretching out his hand for the spade, and said, "Now you rest and let me work."
But the king did not give him the spade and continued to dig. One hour passed, then another. The sun went down behind the trees and at last, the king stuck the spade into the ground and said, "I came to you, wise man, for an answer to my questions. If you can give me no answer, tell me so and I will return home."
Name the lesson and the author.
What were the king's three questions?
Which of the following means 'bed' in the passage:

a plot of ground for plants.
a piece of furniture to sleep on


Why did the king continue to dig?
Find a similar word of 'Flexible'.

Read the following extract and answer the questions that follow:
The king was very happy to have made peace with his enemy so easily, and to have won him over as a friend. He not only forgave him but said he would send his servants and his own doctor to look after him, and he promised to give back the man his property.
Why was the king so happy?
Why do you think the king had forgiven his enemy?
Write any two things that the king promised him.
What does this tell about the king?

Read the following passages carefully and answer the questions that follow each of them:(1x5=5)
When the king had dug two beds, he stopped and repeated his questions. The hermit gave no answer, but stood up, stretching out his hand for the spade, and said, "Now you rest, and let me work."
But the king did not give him the spade and continued to dig. One hour passed, then another. The sun went down behind the trees, and at last the king stuck the spade into the ground and said, "I came to you, wise man, for an answer to my questions. If you can give me no answer, tell me so and I will return home."
"Here comes someone running," said the hermit.
Why did the king go to the wood?
What did he do there?
How long did he work with the spade?
What did the king expect from the hermit ?
Pick out a word from the above passage that means the same as expanding.

Read the following extract and answer the questions that follow:
Leaving the wounded man, the king went out of the hut and looked around for the hermit. Before going away he wished once more to get answers to his questions. The hermit was on his knees sowing seeds in the beds that had been dug the day before. The king went up to the hermit and said, "For the last time I beg you to answer my questions, wise man."
Why did the king look round for the hermit?
What was the hermit doing then?
How did the king address the hermit?
From the passage, find

two adjectives
two pronouns
two common nouns

Read the extract given below and answer the following questions:
You have already been answered! said the hermit still bending down to the ground and looking up at the king as he stood before him. How have I been answered? What do you mean? Do you not see? replied the hermit. If you had not pitied my weakness yesterday and had not dug these beds for me, you would have gone away.
What is being answered in the above lines?
How the king had been answered?
The king had helped the hermit dig the beds. Which question has been answered here?
What would have happened to the king if he would not have helped the hermit?
